KHAS Receives Funding for 6 New Projects Under the TÜBİTAK ARDEB 1001 Program

It is our distinct pleasure to announce that in the 2nd term of 2025 TÜBİTAK ARDEB 1001 program,

6 of the 12 research projects

that submitted from our university have been

funded.

We congratulate our faculty members for their achievement and also we would like to thank our panelists for their valuable contributions during the panels.

The funded project informations are in the table below.

Project Coordinator

Faculty/Department

Project Title

Researcher/Advisor

KHAS Panelists

Assoc. Prof.  Tunçer Baykaş

Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences/Electrical-Electronic Engineering

Form Characteristics and Prosody Evolution in Turkish Music, and Meshk Lineages: Nonlinear Dynamics, and Network Perspectives

Assoc. Prof. Taner Arsan, Asst. Prof. Arif Selçuk Öğrenci, Asst. Prof. Yalçın Şadi

Assoc. Prof. Ayşe Nur Erek

Faculty of Art and Design/Industrial Design

Qualitative analysis of the potential of contemporary art for increasing public and spatial access to urban cultural heritage sites

Assoc. Prof. Defne Karaosmanoğlu (Researcher), Assoc. Prof. Oğuzhan Ceylan (Advisor)

Assoc. Prof. Zeynep Günsür, Assoc. Prof. Balca Arda, Asst. Prof. Perrin Öğün Emre

Assoc. Prof. Sezin Öner Yaman

Faculty of Economics, Administrative and Social Sciences/Psychology

Dynamics of Collective Memory: A Within- and Between-Society Investigation of Narrative Features and Event Phenomenology

Assoc. Prof. Onurcan Yılmaz*

Faculty of Economics, Administrative and Social Sciences/Psychology

The Role of Intuitive and Reflective Processes in Cooperation Under Self-Protection Contexts: A Cross-Cultural, Between- and Within-Subjects Experimental Test of the Generalized Social Heuristics Model

Doç. Dr. Burak Doğruyol (Researcher)

Asst. Prof. Akın Sefer

Core Program

Military Industry, Labour, and Social Transformation in Ottoman Istanbul: The Tophane Zone, 1789-1856

Faculty of Communication / Radio, TV and Cinema

Left Outside of Turkish Cinema: Writing a New Film History Through Alternative Films

Assoc. Prof. Melis Behlil, Assoc. Prof. Defne Karaosmanoğlu, Asst. Prof. Esin Paça Cengiz

*Conditional acceptance.
